components.name: D-Glucose (dextrose)
components.evidence: Overnight cultures of resistant and sensitive cells for each experiment were grown from single colonies and then incubated in sterile Davis Minimal Media with 1,000 g/ml glucose liquid media overnight at 30 C while rotating at 240 rpm. (the '1,000 g/ml' is a typographical error for 1,000 ug/ml = 1 g/L; agar plates used 2,000 ug/ml). The Sigma Davis Minimal Broth base also supplies dextrose at 1.0 g/L.

components.name: Dipotassium phosphate (K2HPO4)
components.source: cited:Sigma Davis Minimal Broth (93753) / BD 275610 / HiMedia M389 datasheet
components.evidence: Davis Minimal Broth Formula per liter: Dextrose 1.0 g; Dipotassium phosphate 7.0 g; Monopotassium phosphate 2.0 g; Sodium citrate 0.5 g; Magnesium sulfate 0.1 g; Ammonium sulfate 1.0 g; Final pH 7.0 +/- 0.2 at 25 C. (paper specifies 'Davis Minimal Media [Sigma]')

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: cited:Sigma Davis Minimal Broth (93753) / BD 275610 / HiMedia M389 datasheet
components.evidence: Davis Minimal Broth Formula per liter: ... Monopotassium phosphate 2.0 g ...

components.name: Ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.source: cited:Sigma Davis Minimal Broth (93753) / BD 275610 / HiMedia M389 datasheet
components.evidence: Davis Minimal Broth Formula per liter: ... Ammonium sulfate 1.0 g ...; 'Ammonium Sulfate is a source of sulfur and nitrogen'.

components.name: Sodium citrate
components.source: cited:Sigma Davis Minimal Broth (93753) / BD 275610 / HiMedia M389 datasheet
components.evidence: Davis Minimal Broth Formula per liter: ... Sodium citrate 0.5 g ... (concentration_mM assumes dihydrate MW 294.1; hydration state not stated on datasheet).

components.name: Magnesium sulfate (MgSO4.7H2O)
components.source: cited:Sigma Davis Minimal Broth (93753) / BD 275610 / HiMedia M389 datasheet
components.evidence: Davis Minimal Broth Formula per liter: ... Magnesium sulfate 0.1 g ...; 'Magnesium is a cofactor for many metabolic reactions' (concentration_mM computed for heptahydrate, MW 246.5, per original Davis & Mingioli 1950 formulation).

components.name: O2 (dissolved oxygen)
components.evidence: Cultures were grown 'while rotating at 240 rpm' and in stirred continuous-culture bioreactors ('constant-volume bacterial cultures (17 mL) are grown in glass vials ... Up to nine cultures can be grown simultaneously using a series of multiposition magnetic stirrers'), i.e. aerated/aerobic conditions.

components.name: Water (H2O)
components.evidence: Suspend 11.6 g in 1000 ml of purified water.

media.base_medium: Davis Minimal Broth (Davis formulation; Davis & Mingioli 1950), Sigma-Aldrich product 93753 / Difco-BD 275610 / HiMedia M389

sources_chased.what: Full-text methods (PMC7266357, PLoS Biol) to identify the medium and its glucose supplement
Commercial Davis Minimal Broth formula (paper cites only 'Sigma' with no literature reference)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch of Sigma-Aldrich 93753 / Davis Minimal Agar 79332 datasheets, BD 275610, and HiMedia M389 datasheet (Sigma/HiMedia PDFs timed out; formula recovered verbatim from datasheet text via search)

media.notes: Davis Minimal Broth is a fully defined minimal medium of six salts plus dextrose; it contains NO added trace-element mix and NO vitamins (E. coli is prototrophic and grows on it), so no hidden stock solution needs expanding and nothing was genuinely missing. The target's current_components already correctly decompose the salts into ions (K, Pi, NH4, SO4, citrate, Mg, Na) plus glucose/O2/H2O/H+; this record adds the compound-level recipe with concentrations. Paper is not anaerobic despite 'facultative' organism label: cultures are shaken (240 rpm) and stirred bioreactors, i.e. aerobic. Glucose '1,000 g/ml' in the paper is a typo for 1,000 ug/ml (1 g/L).
